scope: Quantum SDKs, cloud platforms, hardware platforms, quantum AI, compilers, neutral-atom systems, and annealing ecosystems

rows.buyer: quantum researchers, algorithm developers, enterprise R&D teams, HPC/quantum workflow teams
rows.official_positioning: Open-source SDK for building, optimizing, and executing quantum workflows, with tools for quantum information science, algorithm development, and heterogeneous orchestration.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Qiskit supports serious quantum workflows; Telos can add claim-level proof packets that bind theorem assumptions, circuit fixtures, simulator receipts, hardware metadata, and verifier verdicts.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Use Qiskit-style circuits as inputs while Telos exports portable theorem/circuit/resource receipts across SDKs.

rows.buyer: enterprise quantum teams, researchers, HPC users, hybrid quantum-classical developers
rows.official_positioning: Quantum computing platform combining Azure high-performance computing, AI capabilities, and quantum technologies for scientific discovery and innovation.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Azure Quantum spans cloud, HPC, AI, and quantum workflows; Telos can make assumptions, resource estimates, proof status, and action provenance portable outside one cloud workspace.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Build proof packets that travel with Q#/Azure Quantum jobs, especially when resource estimates or cryptographic-impact claims need auditability.

rows.buyer: researchers, scientists, developers, enterprise teams evaluating multiple quantum hardware modalities
rows.official_positioning: Quantum computing service for researchers and developers to use quantum computers and simulators, build quantum algorithms, and access different hardware types through AWS.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Braket exposes hardware and simulator execution; Telos can bind device choice, calibration metadata, simulator branch, circuit intent, theorem limits, and Crucible verdicts into one proof packet.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Make Braket experiments reviewable as claim packets instead of isolated jobs and notebooks.

rows.buyer: quantum algorithm developers, NISQ researchers, simulator users, hardware-aware quantum programmers
rows.official_positioning: Python library for writing, manipulating, optimizing, and running quantum circuits on quantum computers and simulators, with abstractions for noisy intermediate-scale quantum computers.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Cirq is strong at circuit/program representation; Telos can add theorem packets that distinguish algebraic proof, simulator fixture, noise model, and hardware execution status.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Use Cirq-like circuits as proof-packet fixtures with explicit simulator/hardware branch labels.

rows.buyer: quantum computing researchers, error-correction teams, advanced algorithm teams
rows.official_positioning: Research program focused on developing a large-scale computer capable of complex, error-corrected computations.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Google Quantum AI publishes major research and software; Telos can package external research claims, simulator reproductions, and hardware caveats into buyer-readable proof packets.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Use high-profile quantum results as source anchors while separating verified reproduction, inferred market implications, and unverified scale claims.

rows.buyer: enterprise quantum users, chemistry teams, optimization teams, cybersecurity teams, quantum researchers
rows.official_positioning: Full-stack quantum computing company with trapped-ion quantum computers and software solutions for complex industrial and research problems.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Quantinuum provides hardware and software; Telos can add external proof packets for algorithm claims, device receipts, error assumptions, and cross-platform comparisons.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Turn Quantinuum-style hardware/software runs into portable proof packets with theorem and resource-estimation layers.

rows.buyer: research, development, enterprise quantum, networking, and sensing users
rows.official_positioning: Cloud access to IonQ quantum computers for research, development, and enterprise use, with examples across AWS, Azure, Cirq, and Qiskit.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: IonQ makes hardware accessible through clouds and SDKs; Telos can preserve which cloud, SDK, backend, calibration state, and proof claim the run actually supports.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Make multi-cloud IonQ experiments comparable by normalizing receipts across cloud wrappers.

rows.buyer: quantum hardware researchers, cloud quantum users, superconducting QPU developers
rows.official_positioning: Integrated systems company building quantum computers and superconducting quantum processors, with cloud integration through Quantum Cloud Services.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Rigetti hardware access can support experiments; Telos can add portable proof receipts for device parameters, circuit runs, simulator controls, and theorem limits.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Normalize superconducting QPU experiment evidence into Telos proof packets for cross-backend comparison.

rows.buyer: quantum machine learning, quantum chemistry, differentiable programming, and algorithm research teams
rows.official_positioning: Open-source quantum software platform for quantum computing, quantum machine learning, and quantum chemistry.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: PennyLane is strong at differentiable quantum programming; Telos can add proof packets that bind objective functions, gradients, simulator/hardware branches, seeds, and verifier verdicts.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Make QML experiments auditable with claim-level receipts for data, ansatz, optimizer, backend, and theorem assumptions.

rows.buyer: optimization teams, quantum annealing users, hybrid solver developers
rows.official_positioning: Open-source Python tools for solving hard problems with quantum computers, transforming posed problems into forms suitable for quantum solvers.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Ocean helps formulate and run annealing/hybrid problems; Telos can preserve original problem semantics, transformations, solver settings, objective values, and proof of what was actually optimized.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Use optimization proof packets to distinguish problem formulation correctness from solver performance claims.

rows.buyer: quantum algorithm designers, financial institutions, enterprise quantum teams, hardware-aware circuit developers
rows.official_positioning: Quantum software platform for designing, generating, optimizing, and running sophisticated hardware-aware quantum algorithms and circuits.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Classiq raises abstraction for quantum program synthesis; Telos can bind high-level intent, generated circuit, constraints, resource estimates, and verifier outcomes into one receipt.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Pair compiler/synthesis outputs with proof packets that show exactly which claim the synthesized circuit is allowed to support.

rows.buyer: neutral-atom quantum researchers, cloud users, HPC/quantum infrastructure teams
rows.official_positioning: Provider of quantum computers based on neutral atoms, with a mission to build scalable quantum computers.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Neutral-atom systems introduce modality-specific assumptions; Telos can preserve hardware modality, analog/digital branch, calibration state, and theorem/resource boundaries.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Use neutral-atom runs as evidence sources while clearly separating hardware claims from theorem proofs and simulator fixtures.

rows.buyer: industrial quantum users, HPC/cloud teams, optimization, simulation, and AI-adjacent quantum teams
rows.official_positioning: Quantum computing company developing neutral-atom quantum processors designed to scale toward industrial applications, with HPC and cloud integration.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Pasqal targets industrial neutral-atom applications; Telos can require proof packets that distinguish business problem, quantum model, simulator branch, hardware branch, and result confidence.
rows.telos_wedge_hypothesis: Make industrial quantum pilots auditable by binding problem statements, hardware assumptions, solver receipts, and independent verdicts.
